title: ranunculus arvensis var. tuberculatus
intro: Ranunculus arvensis var. tuberculatus, also known as tubercled lesser celandine, is a variety of the lesser celandine plant, which belongs to the buttercup family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Ranunculus arvensis var. tuberculatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or salve for topical use, or as a tincture or extract for internal use.
